title: Hyper Realistic Sky
description: The biggest, most versatile and feature rich sky pack you will ever find out there.
body:
![banner](https://i.imgur.com/8gXDGTX.png) Hyper Realistic Sky is the biggest, most versatile and feature rich sky pack you will ever find out there. The pack has 8 unique cloud sets that will change depending on the biome you're in. There are also unique skies for all types of weather, when you will usually see dense rain clouds, but when it's snowing, you will see fluffy white snow clouds, and when you're in a desert, you will see sandstorms. Each sky has a day, night, sunset and sunrise variant, as well as a low and high altitude variant, making it a total of 88 cloud variants! On top of that, this pack also features a plethora of special events, such as Solar eclipses, shooting stars, lightning strikes, and even spinning tornadoes. There are many special rare cloud types featured in the pack as well, such as asperitas and mammatus clouds. In addition, this pack also features several skies for the Nether and End dimensions, that will change depending on the biome you're in. Adding it all up, the pack features a total of 114 unique skyboxes! As you can see, this a very large and ambitious project, and the best part is, it's all free! If you like what you see, and would like to support me, you can [donate through PayPal](https://www.paypal.com/donate/?hosted_button_id=ND4XEHFD4XS82). Any and all donations are greatly appreciated. Thank you!
README ### Pick the correct version - **Java vanilla**: These versions are set to "alpha" release type. - **Bedrock**: Download from the [bedrock page](https://www.planetminecraft.com/texture-pack/hyper-realistic-sky-bedrock/). - **Java Modded**: This is the main version of the pack, and is set to "release" type. Select a version and scroll down to see the required mod. If more than one dependency is listed for a version, you only need one. When multiple mods are added as a dependency, that means that the pack will work with all of them, but only one of them is needed to get the pack working. Some mods are available on different versions and mod loaders, so I added support for as many as possible for the widest possible compatibility. Version compatibility is set to the pool of all the mods combined, so what you see is possible to run with at least one of the mods. Check the compatibility table below. - **OptiFine**: This format is supported in the latest release version, and it's mainly intended for people who want to play on the latest Minecraft version with Skyboxify. This is a heavily compromised version of the pack, as many features are impossible to implement the same way as they have been for Nuit and Celestial.
Here is a list of features that are not included:
-rainbow, tornadoes, total solar eclipse, blood moon, blue moon, gas giants, black hole (both in the Overworld and the End), Andromeda, hurricane (outer space variant), seamless fog and the animated sunflare.
And these are features that work somewhat differently due to the format's limitations:
-Large lightning strikes will occur only 4 times per night, and will be animated much more slowly.
-Small lightning strikes will not be visible with Skyboxify, as these are animated sky elements, which Skyboxify doesn't support.
-The Northern star is positioned directly at the horizon, which makes it impossible to see, unless you're at Y>=320.
-Light pollution will only occur at full moon.
-The sun and moon are not properly integrated into the sky layers, which means they will be rendered in front of everything else. - **Light version**: There is a lightweight version of the pack available for download. These versions are set to "beta" release type. Please read their changelogs and check their dependencies. Again, if more than one dependency is listed for a version, you only need one of them. ### FYI - Currently the Nether skies do not work with Nuit. - To check out the many features of the pack, I've provided a text file in the pack that has instructions. - If you want to disable certain featues of the pack, get [Respackopts](https://modrinth.com/mod/respackopts) and use the gizmo in the pack selection screen. Note that if you use Celestial 2.0, the in-game chat will show error messages, but you can ignore those. Alternatively, you can follow the instructions in the text file inside the pack. (this method will not cause error messages to appear) - Celestial 2.0 requires core shaders to have smooth transitions between different sky layers, which is implemented as a pack overlay in the latest release. Since pack overlays only work from 1.20.2 and above, if you're playing on a version below that, you need to copy the "shaders" folder from "celestial/assets/minecraft/" to "assets/minecraft/". ### Shaders This pack does of course work with shaders, but since each shader works differently and has different settings, you will most likely need to tweak them to get a good look.
Features ![Full day cycles](https://i.imgur.com/ZiJlEoX.png) ![Biome dependant skies](https://i.imgur.com/iv0LjgD.png) ![Rain clouds](https://i.imgur.com/XlWTGSN.png) ![Snow clouds](https://i.imgur.com/85zCpV2.png) ![Lightning strikes](https://i.imgur.com/WREBmUS.png) ![High altitude clouds](https://i.imgur.com/Opzeh0R.png) ![Rainbow](https://i.imgur.com/XQmNYVH.png) ![Iridescent clouds](https://i.imgur.com/38dz1dq.png) ![Noctilucent clouds](https://i.imgur.com/cQ6n8mI.png) ![Asperitas clouds](https://i.imgur.com/WXCnHw5.png) ![Mammatus clouds](https://i.imgur.com/SXhYRQ2.png) ![Fiery sunsets](https://i.imgur.com/cBTCfgW.png) ![Sandstorms](https://i.imgur.com/1xZnqbA.png) ![Tornado](https://i.imgur.com/o5sSTsX.png) ![Supercell](https://i.imgur.com/u3XuCEd.png) ![Total Solar Eclipse](https://i.imgur.com/qWyfLXz.png) ![blood moon blue moon](https://i.imgur.com/gp6UXrO.png) ![Gas giants](https://i.imgur.com/vEnREMb.png) ![Auroras](https://i.imgur.com/awJoSVl.png) ![Northern Star](https://i.imgur.com/Xn1IYwu.png) ![Shooting Stars](https://i.imgur.com/u09SF4X.png) ![Meteor shower](https://i.imgur.com/C95Nhil.png) ![Black hole](https://i.imgur.com/EtKCYAE.png) ![Andromeda](https://i.imgur.com/lG5E2EW.png) ![Light pollution](https://i.imgur.com/RSfx0rI.png) ![Outer Space](https://i.imgur.com/cm3W3Xw.png) ![Hurricane](https://i.imgur.com/ioDiU7Y.png) ![Main End sky](https://i.imgur.com/pCFIqNZ.png) ![Custom End flash](https://i.imgur.com/5l0U5TB.png) ![Unique End biome skies](https://i.imgur.com/sch2HF6.png) ![Nether skies](https://i.imgur.com/XKwjxUl.png) ![Nether high altitude](https://i.imgur.com/Od5nVkm.png) ![Proper sky layers](https://i.imgur.com/xVTnXJR.png) ![Seamless fog](https://i.imgur.com/e3r5NBO.png) ![Biome mods support](https://i.imgur.com/JClLnkj.png) ![respackopts support](https://i.imgur.com/AUJJApl.png) ![Vanilla support](https://i.imgur.com/zAb5DjU.png)
Compatibility table ![Compatibility table](https://i.imgur.com/zAQ1Bvr.png)
To see more examples, visit the [Planet Minecraft page](https://www.planetminecraft.com/texture-pack/hyper-realistic-sky/). So that's about it, I hope you'll enjoy this pack!
